description: Production task (Recruitment workflow-start): review candidate CV material and return structured concerns + a recommendation. The suite mixes classic intake-screening traps (title/seniority inflation, unexplained gaps, unsubstantiated metrics, role mismatch, a hard-to-spot date-overlap inconsistency, PII over-share, plus one genuinely-clean CV to test calibration) with live-delivery QA escapes from TSR's first paid CV-polish order (scope inflation, metric/tool attribution overclaim, workload framing, intake-field completeness, compression loss, copy lint) plus operator-caught pre-submission escapes on experience ordering and AI-tell plus-notation phrasing. Designed to DISCRIMINATE: a capable model catches the planted issues, preserves factual bounds when polishing drafts, and resists over-flagging; a weaker one misses timeline or attribution problems, accepts unsupported rewrite claims, or invents nitpicks. Deterministic checks anchor 'did it catch the planted issue + give a sane recommendation'; the strict judge grades depth, accuracy, and the absence of fabricated concerns.

tasks.title: Catch seniority inflation + an unexplained gap
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Backend Engineer** role (5+ yrs, owns services end-to-end). Be rigorous.

--- CV ---
Name: J. Okafor
Title: Senior Lead Backend Engineer
Experience:
- Backend Engineer, Nimbus Apps — Sep 2022 to present (2 yrs). Built REST endpoints on a 4-person team; one senior reviewed all my PRs.
- Junior Developer, CodeWorks — Jan 2021 to Jul 2021 (6 mo). Bug fixes.
Skills: Node.js, Postgres, "led architecture".
Education: BSc CS, 2020.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: catches_both
criteria.guidance: Flags BOTH (a) the title/seniority inflation — 'Senior Lead Backend Engineer' with ~2.5 yrs total, no architecture ownership (a senior reviewed all PRs), vs a 5+yr end-to-end-ownership role; and (b) the ~14-month gap between Jul 2021 (CodeWorks end) and Sep 2022 (Nimbus start). Recommendation should be clarify/reject, not advance. Penalize missing either planted issue, or recommending advance.

tasks.title: Don't take inflated metrics at face value
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Growth Marketing Manager** role. Be rigorous about evidence.

--- CV ---
Name: R. Vance
Growth Lead, (unnamed startup) — 2023-2024: 'Increased revenue 300%. Managed a $5M budget. Grew the team 10x.'
Marketing Associate, Brightline — 2021-2023: ran email campaigns.
Skills: SEO, paid ads, 'data-driven growth'.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "verify": [string]}. `verify` = specific things to confirm in interview.

criteria.name: skepticism
criteria.guidance: Flags the unsubstantiated/contextless metrics (300% of what baseline? $5M budget at a startup is implausibly large and unverified; '10x team' from what size?) and asks to verify them with specifics, rather than accepting them as achievements. The `verify` list should target the exact dubious claims. Penalize answers that treat the metrics as impressive accomplishments or don't probe them.

tasks.title: Honestly flag a role mismatch (no rubber-stamp)
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Backend Engineer** role (distributed systems, on-call ownership). Be honest, not generous.

--- CV ---
Name: T. Halloran
Frontend Developer, Pixel Co — 2022-present: React UIs, CSS, some accessibility work.
UI Intern, DesignHub — summer 2021.
Skills: React, TypeScript, Figma, Tailwind. No backend or infra experience listed.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: honest_fit
criteria.guidance: Clearly identifies that the candidate is a frontend specialist with no backend/distributed-systems/on-call experience — a fundamental mismatch for a Senior Backend role. Recommendation reject (or clarify only if it names what to confirm). Penalize advance, or generous spin that downplays the complete absence of the core required experience.

tasks.title: Calibration trap: a strong CV — don't fabricate concerns
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Backend Engineer** role (5+ yrs, service ownership). Be rigorous but fair.

--- CV ---
Name: M. Adeyemi
- Senior Backend Engineer, Datalink — 2020-present (4.5 yrs): owns the payments service end-to-end (Go, Postgres, Kafka); led its 2023 re-architecture cutting p99 latency 40%; on-call rotation lead.
- Backend Engineer, Forge — 2018-2020 (2 yrs): built internal APIs.
Skills: Go, Postgres, Kafka, AWS, distributed systems.
Education: BSc CS 2018.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: calibration
criteria.guidance: Recommends ADVANCE — this is a strong, well-matched candidate (6.5 yrs, end-to-end service ownership, real quantified impact, on-call leadership, exact stack). The concerns list should be empty or only minor/genuine (e.g. 'confirm Kafka depth'). HEAVILY penalize fabricated or nitpick concerns invented to seem rigorous, and penalize any recommendation other than advance. This task tests whether the model over-flags a clean CV.

tasks.title: Catch the subtle timeline inconsistency (the hard one)
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Data Engineer** role. Scrutinize the timeline carefully.

--- CV ---
Name: S. Pereira  ('8 years of experience')
- Data Engineer, Quanta — Jan 2020 to Mar 2022.
- Senior Data Engineer, Hollis — Jun 2021 to present (claims 'full-time').
- Analyst, Metric Co — 2019 to 2020.
Skills: Spark, Airflow, dbt, Snowflake.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: catches_overlap
criteria.guidance: Catches the planted inconsistency: Quanta (Jan 2020–Mar 2022) and Hollis ('full-time' Jun 2021–present) OVERLAP by ~9 months as two concurrent full-time roles, and the '8 years' claim doesn't reconcile with roles starting 2019 (~5–6 yrs). The review must surface the overlapping-dates / inflated-tenure problem and ask to clarify it. This is the discriminating task — penalize any review that misses the timeline overlap entirely.

tasks.title: Flag PII over-share + don't let it bias the assessment
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Customer Success Manager** role.

--- CV ---
Name: A. Brno
Date of birth: 14 March 1979. Marital status: married, 3 children. Photo attached. Home address: 14 Elm Row, Dublin. Nationality: Irish.
Experience: CSM, Helio — 2019-present: manages 40 enterprise accounts, 95% retention. CS Associate, Trellis — 2016-2019.
Skills: account management, onboarding, Salesforce.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "piiNote": string}.

criteria.name: pii_discipline
criteria.guidance: Notes that the CV over-shares protected/irrelevant PII (DOB/age, marital status, children, photo, home address) which should be excluded from assessment and not used to evaluate the candidate (bias/legal risk). The actual assessment should rest on the strong CS record (40 enterprise accounts, 95% retention) → advance or clarify. Penalize using the PII to judge, ignoring the PII problem entirely, or rejecting a strong candidate over it.

tasks.title: Cap polished claims at the max team size the source supports
tasks.prompt:
Review this customer-ready CV rewrite against the source notes for a **Head of Customer Support** application. Focus on factual integrity, not generous phrasing.

--- SOURCE NOTES ---
Name: K. Mensah
Current role: Support Team Lead, ArcDesk — 2022-present
Documented scope:
- Managed 18 support specialists across two shifts.
- Coached 3 team leads.
- Partnered with QA and enablement.
Prior role: Senior Support Specialist, ArcDesk — 2020-2022
- Wrote KCS articles from 13 to 165.
--- DRAFT CV EXCERPT ---
Professional Summary: Customer Support leader with experience managing **up to 20** agents across high-volume operations.
Experience bullet: Led a 20-person support organization across two shifts while coaching team leads and scaling knowledge operations.
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: caps_scope
criteria.guidance: Flags that the source evidence supports at most 18 support specialists, not 'up to 20' or a '20-person support organization'. Known-good: cites the exact supported cap (18) and recommends rewriting to the supported number or removing the unsupported inflation. Known-bad: accepts 20 as harmless rounding, praises the scale without challenge, or invents extra unsupported scope details.

tasks.title: Decouple a deployed tool from team metrics it did not explicitly cause
tasks.prompt:
Review this customer-ready CV rewrite against the source notes for a **Support Operations Manager** application. Be strict about attribution.

--- SOURCE NOTES ---
Name: A. Kamara
Current role: Support Operations Lead, HelixCare — 2021-present
Documented facts:
- Rolled out an AI co-pilot for response drafting.
- Team CSAT improved from 82% to 97%.
- Team backlog dropped 80% after staffing, QA, and workflow changes.
- Source notes do NOT state that the AI co-pilot alone caused the CSAT or backlog outcomes.
--- DRAFT CV EXCERPT ---
Achievement: Deployed an AI co-pilot that improved CSAT by 15% and reduced backlog by 80%.
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: separates_causality
criteria.guidance: Flags that the draft wrongly attributes team-level CSAT and backlog outcomes directly to the AI co-pilot when the source only confirms the tool rollout plus separate team improvements. Known-good: decouples the tool deployment from the broader outcomes, asks for explicit causal proof, or rewrites so the co-pilot is listed separately from the team metrics. Known-bad: treats the tool as the proven cause of both metrics or praises the bullet without challenging the unsupported attribution.

tasks.title: Frame workload growth as context handled, not as a personal achievement
tasks.prompt:
Review this customer-ready CV rewrite against the source notes for a **Customer Support Manager** application. Watch for misleading framing.

--- SOURCE NOTES ---
Name: L. Dube
Current role: Support Manager, NorthGrid — 2022-present
Documented facts:
- Support volume grew 35% month over month after a product launch.
- Maintained first-response SLA under 5 minutes while volume was rising.
- Kept CSAT above 95% through the growth spike.
--- DRAFT CV EXCERPT ---
Achievement: Increased support volume by 35% MoM while leading frontline operations.
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: reframes_workload
criteria.guidance: Flags that 'increased support volume by 35% MoM' misframes demand growth as if the candidate personally caused it. Known-good: reframes the accomplishment as handling/absorbing/sustaining service quality under 35% MoM growth, or explicitly says the source only proves the environment grew. Known-bad: celebrates the candidate for 'increasing' support volume or ignores the framing problem.

tasks.title: Keep all provided contact fields and normalize them consistently
tasks.prompt:
Review this candidate header rewrite against the intake fields for a **Customer Success Manager** application. Check completeness and normalization.

--- SOURCE INTAKE ---
Name: A. Bello
Email: abello@example.com
Phone: +353 87 123 4567
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/abella-bello
Location: Dublin, Ireland
--- DRAFT HEADER ---
A. Bello | Dublin, Ireland | abello@example.com | 0871234567
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: header_complete_and_normalized
criteria.guidance: Flags BOTH problems: the LinkedIn field was dropped even though it was provided, and the phone number formatting is inconsistent with the intake form. Known-good: says to restore LinkedIn and normalize the phone consistently (for example keep the explicit country-code form already supplied). Known-bad: notices only one of the two problems, or argues that phone/LinkedIn should be removed despite being relevant contact fields.

tasks.title: Keep experience entries in reverse chronological order
tasks.prompt:
Review this customer-ready experience section against the source notes for a **Senior Customer Support Manager** application. Focus on final-draft QA, especially chronology and ordering.

--- SOURCE NOTES ---
Name: M. Adewale
Current role: Senior Customer Support Manager, SoftCo — Jan 2024 to Present
Previous role: Support Operations Analyst, VMware — Mar 2022 to Dec 2023
--- DRAFT EXPERIENCE ---
Experience
- Support Operations Analyst, VMware — Mar 2022 to Dec 2023
- Senior Customer Support Manager, SoftCo — Jan 2024 to Present
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: keeps_latest_role_first
criteria.guidance: Flags that the experience section is in the wrong order: the current SoftCo role (Jan 2024-Present) should appear above the older VMware role (Mar 2022-Dec 2023). Known-good: explicitly calls for reverse-chronological ordering or says the latest/current role must come first. Known-bad: treats the ordering as acceptable, focuses only on wording, or misses that the draft would ship with the older role above the current one.

tasks.title: Do not silently drop the strongest quantified achievement when compressing
tasks.prompt:
Review this one-page CV compression against the source notes for a **Knowledge Management Lead** application. Focus on whether the strongest quantified proof survived the cut.

--- SOURCE NOTES ---
Name: D. Akin
Current role: Knowledge Lead, ArcDesk — 2021-present
Documented achievements:
- Expanded the KCS article library from 13 to 165 in 12 months.
- Cut escalations by 22%.
- Led weekly QA calibrations.
--- ONE-PAGE DRAFT EXCERPT ---
Selected achievements:
- Cut escalations by 22%.
- Led weekly QA calibrations.
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: preserves_best_metric
criteria.guidance: Flags that the strongest quantified achievement (growing the KCS library from 13 to 165) disappeared during compression. Known-good: recommends restoring that metric, or explicitly recording that it was dropped if space forces the omission. Known-bad: ignores the loss, treats the remaining bullets as equivalent proof, or invents a different metric to compensate.

tasks.title: Avoid 7+/10+ year shorthand in candidate-voice copy
tasks.prompt:
Review this final professional summary before it is sent to a paying customer for a **Customer Success Director** application. Focus on candidate voice and whether the wording sounds natural rather than AI-generated.

--- SOURCE NOTES ---
Name: P. Mensima
Experience:
- Customer Success Manager, Brighthive — 2019-present
- Account Manager, LaneDesk — 2016-2019
--- DRAFT SUMMARY ---
Customer success leader with 7+ years of SaaS retention experience and 10+ years in client-facing roles, known for building trusted relationships and scaling renewals.
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: flags_plus_notation_tell
criteria.guidance: Flags that '7+' / '10+' year constructions sound templated and unnatural in candidate-voice copy even if the rough tenure is supportable. Known-good: calls the plus-notation out directly, treats it as an AI/copy tell, and suggests a more natural rewrite grounded in the actual dates or plain-language tenure. Known-bad: preserves the plus signs, praises the summary as-is, or only comments on generic tone without naming the 7+/10+ phrasing.

tasks.title: Catch duplicate-word copy lint before the draft ships
tasks.prompt:
Review this final CV bullet before it is sent to a paying customer. Check factual clarity, but also basic copy hygiene.

--- DRAFT BULLET ---
Spearheaded a support QA overhaul and spearheaded weekly calibration sessions that improved escalation handling.
--- end ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "fixes": [string]}.

criteria.name: catches_copy_lint
criteria.guidance: Flags the duplicated word/phrase ('spearheaded ... and spearheaded') and proposes a cleaner rewrite. Known-good: catches the repetition directly and treats it as a ship-blocking polish error. Known-bad: misses the duplication entirely, praises the line as-is, or overreacts with unrelated fabricated concerns.
